summary: This is a brief link-roundup post from early in Joel's blogging career. He urges readers to contact their senators about a flag desecration amendment vote, links to his own article about NDAs and contracts to avoid signing, and recommends Jim Clark's article on dividing startup equity fairly. The post is more of a curated collection of timely links than a substantive essay.
key_points: Joel advocates against a constitutional amendment to outlaw flag desecration, directing readers to the ACLU for action
He references his own writing on NDAs and contracts that startup employees should never sign
He highlights the common startup problem of dividing ownership fairly and recommends Jim Clark's article as the best treatment of the topic
money_quotes: An issue which comes up a lot in start up companies is how to divide up the ownership fairly.
key_insight: Dividing startup equity fairly is a perennial challenge, and founders should educate themselves on both ownership splits and the contracts they sign.
